question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the network to induce a TCP-level partition between the importer and the database mid-execution of prune(), causing the synchronized lock to be held indefinitely by a hung DB call, thereby starving all future scheduled invocations and achieving a persistent denial-of-service on the retention scheduler?
